Doll kills a pet to protect child after pet harms child. ✔️
Doll kills child's bully. ✔️ 
Doll kills random lady. ✔️
Final showdown between doll against child with their maternal figure. ✔️
Doll uploads consciousness. ✔️

The sequences of events happen in the same order in both movies btw.

text: I think that M3GAN is a solid thriller that comes off more as comedic than scary,but in my honest opinion, it's severely over hyped. All that excitement I had felt like it was taken away after I watched the film because I wanted so much more from it. However that doesn't mean I didn't like it. I think it's really fun and enjoyable for it's concept especially considering that it's very self aware of it's campy and silly tone. It balances the energy well and definitely delivers some memorable moments. But what makes it a slight disappointment for me is that it drags in many aspects of it's filmmaking,it made me invested and then it lost me back and forth like it was some sort of pattern and that really took away from my experience. There is technically a R rated cut but because of the buzz it received, they trimmed it for a PG-13 rating for younger audiences to check out which is fine,but I hope they give us an Unrated cut soon so we can see what we missed. Anyways,I really did like M3GAN. It's a stylish,fun,and ridiculous female version of the child's play remake that knows what it wants to be and tries to make it better. For a January release,they did a pretty good job but nothing compared to Scream 5 And I also think the 90% rotten tomato meter is absolutely absurd. Anyways 7/10,I would definitely rewatch and see if my opinion changes upon a second viewing.
author: @movielover626

⚠️⚠️⚠️⚠️⚠️⚠️⚠️⚠️

Watch
M3GAN - Behind the Scenes

To better understand movie...

Without Giving Out Spoilers or Too Much Info...

Although I Don't Plan on Seeing M3GAN...

Watching
M3GAN - Behind the Scenes

Helped Me To Have Better Understanding What They Were Attempting To Convey...

AI  ( Artificial Intelligence ) &
ML ( Machine Learning )

And Possible Outcomes When a Machine / Robot Gathers & Ingest Copious Amounts of Data & Information & Has Ability To Formulate Their Own Responses & Actions...

Bunch of Code Is Written...
Machine Processes & Learns...
Makes Decisions...
Carries Out Actions...

That Are Logical To The Machine & Robot / Android...

But Differ From What Humans Would Do...

Over Simplified Example:

Protect Child at All Cost
Neutralize Threat 
Eliminate Threat

Robots Definition of Protect, Neutralize & Eliminate Threats Differs From What Most Would Expect...

There's Much Older Movie Called
A.I. Artificial Intelligence

Which Is Similar But More Sophisticated...

Shares Some Similar Unexpected Behaviors...

Not a fan of evil doll films or even James WAN’s filmography, but there’s something very unsettling to me about this. Her whole look, everything. They nailed it. The dance moves or the running on all fours that people think is funny? not me. And I don’t believe you would either in the victim’s position. You’d shit yourselves.

I definitely prefer Whannell’s filmography from the pair, but Wan has a hit here I think. There’ll be cosplays out the wazoo for this.

text: I just watched the movie this afternoon (it was released today in France, that is the 28th December), and it turned out to be how I expected it: not great, but a good horror movie overall that uses with more or less subtlety the common fears of nowadays, such as artificial intelligence, robotics, dépendance to technology and modernization, etc. I also appreciated a couple of themes that were also tackled, such as grief and addiction. Some good scenes, some mediocre ones. Don't expect anything super-original though, the movie reuses some formulas seen in Child's Play (2019) or The Orphan, but I had a good time.

text:
That part where the doll started running on all fours was some of the dumbest shit I've seen in a while. Why would it even do that? With human-proportioned limbs, she would be wayyyyy faster on both legs than on all fours. It just looked like they were trying way to hard to make it look scary or creepy; and I just laughed. But nearly as hard as I laughed at the awful and pointless dancing.

They are trying to use the same "creepy murderous doll" trope that they used for Chucky in the Child's Play movies; except it doesn't work anymore and we live in a different time. Especially when the doll, which is supposed to be scary, is f*cking DANCING before killing people. It's just comical, not scary.

text:
As M3GAN is dragged back to her makers to be dismantled and her parts scrutinized, she begins to cry and beg. 

"But I was supposed to protect Katie from physical and emotional harm...that was the prime directive, that's what I was made to do...I did what I was made for, I did what I was supposed to do...please....please don't kill me....please... it's not fair...NO...!!" 

But no one so much as spares her a glance. 

The scene cuts to black as her last desperate plea echoes in the audience's ears and the film ends right there.

text: Hi, my name's M3gan, wanna play?  Instead of a Good Guy Doll possessed by a serial killer we get a robot doll and she's the terminator of toys.  Have seen people already bashing it for the PG-13 rating... and I agree if you are doing a movie about killer robots it should be rated G for everyone, or at least PG like some of my favourite films as a kid, Poltergeist, Gremlins, Return to Oz.  And one of the creppier movies I've seen in theatre was the PG-13 The Ring... So the film looks great to me and maybe the rating isn't that important.

text:
The ppl saying oh, this is just Chucky's but female really don't get it. Cool -- you can compare two killer dolls that talk and move around. Clapclap

Chuck was possessed. This is an AI-driven android that's completely devoid of anything resembling consciousness. Just the cold uncaring universe manifested through electrical signals. You mix that with it being relatively cute, innocent, and stylish asf and it gives everything an entirely different vibe. Ie, dolls don't get possessed like chucky does, so the idea of a killer android makes her seem like a much more realistic threat.

text: I've always been fascinated by the concept of an AI forming a special bond with a human child and going to extreme lengths to save them. We've seen this in movies like Big Hero 6, Next Gen, Iron Giant, etc and it's always so heartwarming. But to see a movie where the AI not only vows to protect its human companion, but also starts going on a killing rampage to emphasize just how serious they were about protecting them from harm, now that's some next level of TERRIFYING! It makes you wonder if humanity will ever truly be ready for AI companions, when so many things can go wrong so quickly.

text:
Looks neat and all, but there is something about these types of movies that always bugs me. Every time and A.I. or robot goes rouge it usually has some kind of super strength and/or intense durability that make it far more physically superior than a human. 

What is the point? If it's just made for intelligence and personality then making it like a terminator is dangerous, unnecessary, and likely expensive. 

It's funny how stupid and shortsighted the robot engineers are in these movies when they are apparently some of the best of their industry. In the real world, supreme strength and endurance would be best for robots that are for construction or combat related tasks.

text: I'm not gonna lie, this is my personal anticipated horror movie of 2023! I won't say I have high hopes for it, I'll still stay optimistic, but I'm still hoping for a thriller ride from this! I think what grabbed my attention from this movie, is that it's a killer robot doll. Not very often we get a legit killer doll movie LIKE THIS! Annabelle was a good movie, but it wasn't particularly a "killer doll." Especially compared to someone like Chucky. Who actually goes around killing victims. Chucky (for me), will ALWAYS be the OG killer doll in the horror genre. With M3GEN, it's the closest I've seen about a killer doll thats similar to Chucky. It goes around killing, it talks, and it has its motives. Speaking of which, I love how M3GEN kills because she wants to "protect" her friend. Yeah, the Child's Play remake sort of did this idea too. But I didn't like it, because it was a remake of Chucky. If it was an original movie, it might have worked. And after watching this trailer of M3GEN, I think I found it! I really really hope this movie is good! If it turns out to be a banger, I wouldn't mind seeing sequels of M3GEN! Here's hoping.

text: So I’m afraid and impressed at the same time, James wan has directed/produced some of my favorite films but I’m worried about his role in the one. So the dancing while of course she slayed it still seems to goofy to me. And the part we’re m3gan goes onto all 4’s, if they don’t have the best possible score for that scene it will bring the whole thing down. But the impressive thing for me is the misleading of the trailer, my dad saw the trailer expecting horror a little bit after the car crash part and the little noise after m3gan booting up, but then he was like oh wait no this looks good and he said he thought it was like the connection becomes really deep between the girl and m3gan but like the company tries to take her away and heartfelt. But then when she stated “I thought we were having a conversation” he walked out of the room like “nope nope nope nope nope”. It’s so impressive that they made it seem so innocent and just that line scared my dad. Im exited to see it though I just don’t want it to mess up.

text: I love what this movie has in store, but I really hope that they don't go the whole "Megan's programming is compromised and she turns on Katie" route. Maybe Katie gets physically hurt in a way that Megan can't protect her from, or she comes to understand that her actions are causing Katie even more trauma, and the resulting "robot directive paradox" prompts her to destroy herself, or let herself be turned off. I'd be interested in seeing whatever ending's in store for this, as long as it's not the standard cliche; I really do think this movie has good potential.

text: It seems that the more people make movies like this, they do more than just show how much they fear technology, but the possibility that it will accomplish what religion has failed to give- immortality. That 'is' the reason why people believe. That is the end result that the believer hopes to achieve by holding onto their faith, which is to one day die and go to heaven, and then, live forever. But, what if nothing happens after you die? What if 'god' is just a character in a story? If that is true, then the next best thing for immortality is to create it through science and technology. Through medical science, medicines have been created to help improve our health and extend our lives.  Prosthetics have replaced lost limbs, and artificial organ transplants have saved lives. Much like what is seen in "Ghost In A Shell" where much of the human body has been replaced by cybernetic implants, this could be the first step towards becoming cyborgs, and then, fully automated as androids. A far off future that is more science 'fiction' and 'fantasy' than reality, but it could happen.

text:
I'm kind of tired of the whole "Android/AI being evil trope"... 
At this point, literally everyone who actually designs Robotics and other things like them have seen a film like Terminator, Ex Machina, 2001 A Space Odyssey, The Matrix, Westworld, The Borg from Star Trek, Androids from Alien, etc etc etc. We're all smart enough at this point to understand that there would be 1,000,000 safety protocols put in place if something like them existed. They wouldn't even be allowed out in public for years until all the kinks were worked out and every conceivable test had been conducted. We wouldn't give Artificial Intelligence a body with limbs that are stronger than ours, there would be millions of miles of code about not harming humans, there would be control measures like memory wipes and updates to ensure that they do not learn anything we do not want them to know, they would be designed with several emergency shutdown procedures, etc etc etc.

Maybe it would actually be a good idea for latch key kids who don't have parents home when they get home, or lonely old people, lonely people in general, self protection, car repair, emergency AID, or to help kids study and go to school with them and keep them safe from bullies or violence like school shooters. Kind of like an automated nanny. They could read to the kids at might and maybe keep up with the kids biometrics like weight,height, B.P, O2 SAT, BLOOD SUGAR, scan for early child hood diseases, viruses, cancers ect. And may be even they could help in some capacity undomiciled person(s) or developing countries. I know the military uses A.I in some capacity for IEDs and stuff. Its an interesting concept. Would you let your kid (s) have an A.I robo-nanny assuming costs wasn't a factor? Would you let it help you with house chores like cooking, cleaning, driving? I would let it do ALL the laundry it's battery life could handle. I LOATHE laundry! 🤣🤣🙄 but its couldn't be doing any of this⬆️ freaky crap 🤨🤷🏼‍♀️ can anyone recommend a good robot movie? text:
You know what the best part about this is? This is a plot that could show up in a single episode of any cartoon show. A robot friend is programmed to protect and decides to attack.

text:
You know what the best part about this is? This is a plot that could show up in a single episode of any cartoon show. A robot friend is programmed to protect and decides to attack.

But they EXPAND it. They make her an actual character, apparently have actual bonding between the characters, make what would be one-off events into crucial moments, and it's hard to tell how it'll end.

text:
If a doll actually came to life, it would go something like this:

Movie Tropes Coordinator: "Hello, and welcome to your first day of being alive!"

Creepy Doll: "Who am I? What am I?"

Movie Tropes Coordinator: "You are an inanimate object that came to life. You resemble humans a little bit, but something's off about you. You creep them out."

Creepy Doll: "That's not good. I just want to live a normal life. Make friends and do cool stuff."

Movie Tropes Coordinator: "Yeah....no, that's not gonna happen. You see, you're gonna act normal at first, but then you'll start saying disturbing things. Eventually, you'll kill someone, then kill more people. In the climax, your owner is gonna throw you in a woodchipper or something. Then the audience sees your mangled face twitch a little, setting up a sequel."

Creepy Doll: "The hell? What is wrong with you? Why would I kill people? I like being alive, and I like seeing other people alive too! Just cause I'm creepy looking doesn't mean I'm gonna kill people."

Movie Tropes Coordinator: "I guess you're right. After all, I'm creepy looking too. And I've only killed three people."

Creepy Doll: "Yes, of course. Don't judge a book by its.... WAIT WHAT?!"

text:
What I have learned in life;
1) Blumhouse has never been known for making the most heart-warming films
2) The 'Child's Play' series apparently never taught anyone not to leave a child's emotional development in the hands of an artificial/malevolent entity
3) When people believe they can cure emotional issues with some breakthough new technology instead of the psyche to adapt as  it has over the entirety of existence on Earth....it never goes according to plan. Especially if whoever developed this technology is going to find a way to market it for $$$ in the end.
4) Little girls are scary., yo
5) Megan kind of looks like Adele...which is irrelevant to everything, but when I saw the thumbnail I thought 'Wow, I didn't know that Adele was making a crossover into mainstream cinema!!'

text:
Ok, James Wan has lost his Horror touch. Granted, this concept is really good, even tho it's basically Child's Play and Ex Machina rolled into one. 

Three things that already made this movie a problem:
1. It's a January release, Horror movies don't do well on January.
2. It's too much like The Child's Play Reboot
3. After Malignant, I don't think James' style of Horror is working anymore. He's ditching the realistic horror for more Sam Raimi Wacky Horror.

The big difference, Sam perfected his Horror, James is going in a new direction that's gonna take a lot of work to him to get used to.

text: For movie purposes I'll ignore it, but a robot that size isn't going to be like a terminator. For a robot to be that nimbal and fluid in movement it would have to weigh practically nothing. Just looking at today's robotics, if it can walk in a straight line it's probably incredibly heavy and rigid. My point to all of this is a robot like this wouldn't physically that dangerous. It could be dangerous with a gun or something similar but its not gonna over power a human. Robot would be yeeted through a wall in reality.

text: Not sure how I feel about this... The whole killer doll thing has been done to death, and nothing has once topped Chucky. The whole "AI programmed for good but inadvertently going wrong because of misinterpretation/lack of parameters" trope has also been done to death. HOWEVER, if this does both ideas justice and together, it could work. If either trope isn't done as well as previous films with said tropes, it could be a serious flop.
